title: alchemilla vulgaris subsp. straminea
intro: Alchemilla vulgaris subsp. straminea, commonly known as the golden lady's mantle, is a subspecies of the lady's mantle plant, characterized by its golden-yellow flowers and narrow leaves. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can aid in wound healing and reduce pain. The health benefits of Alchemilla vulgaris subsp. straminea include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce menstrual cramps, and alleviate symptoms of menopausal hot flashes. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, with the leaves and flowers being the primary parts used in herbal preparations.
